title: 5-letter words: speed test
description: Longer common words — push the 5-letter speed barrier
text: about after again being below could every first found great might never other place right small sound still their there these thing think three those today under water world write

tips.text: Longer words make your hands trade off, left then right. That hand-off is where speed actually lives.
Don't glance down, not even on the awkward ones. Look down and you lose the thought before you've set it.

tips.target: about after again being below could every first found great might never other place right

tips.text: An even pace beats a fast one with potholes. On deadline, the steady typist always finished first.

tips.target: small sound still their there these thing think three those today under water world write

character_tips.anna: Hands trading the work is your best lift in speed. Feel them keep time together. - Eleanor Hayes, editor
character_tips.maxim: Five letters covers most real words. Settle into the rhythm and hold it. - Eleanor Hayes, editor
character_tips.knopych: A little longer now. Same calm hands, same even beat. - Eleanor Hayes, editor
character_tips.klavochka: Both hands in step, neither one rushing the other. - Eleanor Hayes, editor
